target.name: Transmutex
target.founder: Franklin Servan-Schreiber, Federico Carminati, Jean-Pierre Revol

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Transmutex, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Servan-Schreiber, Carminati, and Revol, is evaluated against a peer universe of 11 companies across the nuclear sector—3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, and Kairos Power. The company has raised $23M to date.

The analysis applies stage-progression valuation, the single methodologically sound approach given data constraints. Using the $23M total raised as a valuation proxy, a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression yields $46M. A peer-median approach was not applicable: while 8 same-sector private peers exist, insufficient datapoints within narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, similar-tier) precluded reliable median extraction; relying on all-sector median would introduce unwarranted noise.

The triangulation supports a median valuation of $46M, with a low estimate of $39M and high estimate of $53M, reflecting confidence bounds around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include technology commercialization risk inherent to frontier nuclear ventures, the nascent regulatory environment for advanced reactors, and capital intensity requirements for scaling. The narrow peer set and single-method approach underscores dependence on comparable funding-round dynamics rather than revenue or profit metrics—typical for pre-revenue frontier-tech but a material limitation for valuation robustness.
